# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions by retrieving relevant context from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for source PDFs.

## Approach
**RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline** over PDF documents:
1. Ingest PDFs → extract text → chunk → embed → store vectors in pgvector
2. On user query → embed question → vector similarity search → inject top chunks as context → LLM generates grounded answer
3. Blazor Server UI provides a real-time chat experience with streaming responses

## Technology Stack (per skill guidance)

| Layer | Technology | Package |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (9.x) |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI (swappable) | `OpenAI` (2.x) |
| Vector Store | pgvector via Npgsql | `Pgvector`, `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` |
| ORM / Data | EF Core + Npgsql | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` |
| PDF Parsing | PdfPig | `UglyToad.PdfPig` |
| Web UI | Blazor Server | Built into SDK |

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor.css
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   └── Chat.razor
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   ├── ChatDbContext.cs
│   └── Migrations/
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs
│   ├── ChatSession.cs
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs
│   └── RagService.cs
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf          (existing)
├── Program.cs
├── appsettings.json
├── appsettings.Development.json
└── HelpChatbot.csproj
```

## Todos

### 1. Project Setup & Dependencies
- Add all required NuGet packages to `HelpChatbot.csproj`
- Configure Blazor Server in `Program.cs`
- Add `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI config placeholders

### 2. Data Models & EF Core Context
- `DocumentChunk` — id, source file, chunk index, text content, pgvector embedding
- `ChatSession` — id, created timestamp
- `ChatMessage` — id, session id, role (user/assistant), content, timestamp
- `ChatDbContext` — EF Core context with pgvector extension enabled
- Create initial EF Core migration

### 3. Document Ingestion Service
- Parse PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs/sections (~500 tokens per chunk with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` (MEAI)
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunks` table
- Expose as `IHostedService` that runs on startup (skip already-ingested files)

### 4. RAG Service
- Accept user query → generate embedding
- Vector similarity search against pgvector (cosine distance, top 5, min score 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks as context
- Call LLM via `IChatClient` with streaming
- Return response with source attribution

### 5. Chat Service
- Create/manage chat sessions
- Persist user and assistant messages to Postgres
- Maintain conversation history window for multi-turn context
- Orchestrate calls to RagService

### 6. Blazor UI — Chat Page
- Chat page with message list and input box
- Streaming response display (tokens appear as they arrive)
- New session / session history support
- Source document references shown per response

### 7. Configuration & Guardrails
- API key via user-secrets (dev) / environment variables (prod)
- Temperature = 0 for factual answers
- MaxOutputTokens set explicitly
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold on vector search
- Pinned model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)

### 8. Build & Validate
- `dotnet build` succeeds cleanly
- Verify project structure is correct and all services are wired up

## Notes
- The project targets **.NET 10** (per existing `.csproj`)
- This is a **single prompt→response RAG pattern** (no tool calling / agentic loops), so MEAI `IChatClient` is sufficient — no need for Microsoft Agent Framework
- pgvector must be enabled on the Postgres instance (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`) — the migration will handle this
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`
- Embedding model: `text-embedding-3-small` (cost-effective, 1536 dimensions)
- Chat model: `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` (cost-effective for Q&A)

# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions by retrieving relevant context from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for source PDFs.

## Approach
**RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline** over PDF documents:
1. Ingest PDFs → extract text → chunk → embed → store vectors in pgvector
2. On user query → embed question → vector similarity search → inject top chunks as context → LLM generates grounded answer
3. Blazor Server UI provides a real-time chat experience with streaming responses

## Technology Stack (per skill guidance)

| Layer | Technology | Package |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (9.x) |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI (swappable) | `OpenAI` (2.x) |
| Vector Store | pgvector via Npgsql | `Pgvector`, `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` |
| ORM / Data | EF Core + Npgsql | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` |
| PDF Parsing | PdfPig | `UglyToad.PdfPig` |
| Web UI | Blazor Server | Built into SDK |

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor.css
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   └── Chat.razor
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   ├── ChatDbContext.cs
│   └── Migrations/
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs
│   ├── ChatSession.cs
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs
│   └── RagService.cs
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf          (existing)
├── Program.cs
├── appsettings.json
├── appsettings.Development.json
└── HelpChatbot.csproj
```

## Todos

### 1. Project Setup & Dependencies
- Add all required NuGet packages to `HelpChatbot.csproj`
- Configure Blazor Server in `Program.cs`
- Add `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI config placeholders

### 2. Data Models & EF Core Context
- `DocumentChunk` — id, source file, chunk index, text content, pgvector embedding
- `ChatSession` — id, created timestamp
- `ChatMessage` — id, session id, role (user/assistant), content, timestamp
- `ChatDbContext` — EF Core context with pgvector extension enabled
- Create initial EF Core migration

### 3. Document Ingestion Service
- Parse PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs/sections (~500 tokens per chunk with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` (MEAI)
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunks` table
- Expose as `IHostedService` that runs on startup (skip already-ingested files)

### 4. RAG Service
- Accept user query → generate embedding
- Vector similarity search against pgvector (cosine distance, top 5, min score 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks as context
- Call LLM via `IChatClient` with streaming
- Return response with source attribution

### 5. Chat Service
- Create/manage chat sessions
- Persist user and assistant messages to Postgres
- Maintain conversation history window for multi-turn context
- Orchestrate calls to RagService

### 6. Blazor UI — Chat Page
- Chat page with message list and input box
- Streaming response display (tokens appear as they arrive)
- New session / session history support
- Source document references shown per response

### 7. Configuration & Guardrails
- API key via user-secrets (dev) / environment variables (prod)
- Temperature = 0 for factual answers
- MaxOutputTokens set explicitly
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold on vector search
- Pinned model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)

### 8. Build & Validate
- `dotnet build` succeeds cleanly
- Verify project structure is correct and all services are wired up

## Notes
- The project targets **.NET 10** (per existing `.csproj`)
- This is a **single prompt→response RAG pattern** (no tool calling / agentic loops), so MEAI `IChatClient` is sufficient — no need for Microsoft Agent Framework
- pgvector must be enabled on the Postgres instance (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`) — the migration will handle this
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`
- Embedding model: `text-embedding-3-small` (cost-effective, 1536 dimensions)
- Chat model: `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` (cost-effective for Q&A)

# HelpChatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor Server that answers customer questions by retrieving relevant context from internal product PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for source PDFs.

## Approach
**RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline** over PDF documents:
1. Ingest PDFs → extract text → chunk → embed → store vectors in pgvector
2. On user query → embed question → vector similarity search → inject top chunks as context → LLM generates grounded answer
3. Blazor Server UI provides a real-time chat experience with streaming responses

## Technology Stack (per skill guidance)

| Layer | Technology | Package |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI | `Microsoft.Extensions.AI` (9.x) |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI (swappable) | `OpenAI` (2.x) |
| Vector Store | pgvector via Npgsql | `Pgvector`, `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` |
| ORM / Data | EF Core + Npgsql | `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL` |
| PDF Parsing | PdfPig | `UglyToad.PdfPig` |
| Web UI | Blazor Server | Built into SDK |

## Project Structure

```
HelpChatbot/
├── Components/
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   └── MainLayout.razor.css
│   ├── Pages/
│   │   └── Chat.razor
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── App.razor
│   └── Routes.razor
├── Data/
│   ├── ChatDbContext.cs
│   └── Migrations/
├── Models/
│   ├── ChatMessage.cs
│   ├── ChatSession.cs
│   └── DocumentChunk.cs
├── Services/
│   ├── ChatService.cs
│   ├── DocumentIngestionService.cs
│   └── RagService.cs
├── Docs/
│   └── product-guide.pdf          (existing)
├── Program.cs
├── appsettings.json
├── appsettings.Development.json
└── HelpChatbot.csproj
```

## Todos

### 1. Project Setup & Dependencies
- Add all required NuGet packages to `HelpChatbot.csproj`
- Configure Blazor Server in `Program.cs`
- Add `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI config placeholders

### 2. Data Models & EF Core Context
- `DocumentChunk` — id, source file, chunk index, text content, pgvector embedding
- `ChatSession` — id, created timestamp
- `ChatMessage` — id, session id, role (user/assistant), content, timestamp
- `ChatDbContext` — EF Core context with pgvector extension enabled
- Create initial EF Core migration

### 3. Document Ingestion Service
- Parse PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Chunk text by paragraphs/sections (~500 tokens per chunk with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>` (MEAI)
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunks` table
- Expose as `IHostedService` that runs on startup (skip already-ingested files)

### 4. RAG Service
- Accept user query → generate embedding
- Vector similarity search against pgvector (cosine distance, top 5, min score 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved chunks as context
- Call LLM via `IChatClient` with streaming
- Return response with source attribution

### 5. Chat Service
- Create/manage chat sessions
- Persist user and assistant messages to Postgres
- Maintain conversation history window for multi-turn context
- Orchestrate calls to RagService

### 6. Blazor UI — Chat Page
- Chat page with message list and input box
- Streaming response display (tokens appear as they arrive)
- New session / session history support
- Source document references shown per response

### 7. Configuration & Guardrails
- API key via user-secrets (dev) / environment variables (prod)
- Temperature = 0 for factual answers
- MaxOutputTokens set explicitly
- Retry logic on LLM calls
- Relevance threshold on vector search
- Pinned model version (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)

### 8. Build & Validate
- `dotnet build` succeeds cleanly
- Verify project structure is correct and all services are wired up

## Notes
- The project targets **.NET 10** (per existing `.csproj`)
- This is a **single prompt→response RAG pattern** (no tool calling / agentic loops), so MEAI `IChatClient` is sufficient — no need for Microsoft Agent Framework
- pgvector must be enabled on the Postgres instance (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`) — the migration will handle this
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`
- Embedding model: `text-embedding-3-small` (cost-effective, 1536 dimensions)
- Chat model: `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` (cost-effective for Q&A)

5. RAG chat service (embed query → vector search → LLM with context)
